mainEntity.name: How long does it take to fix dog reactivity?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most owners see meaningful change inside the first few weeks of a structured programme. A full transformation typically takes 60 days of consistent in-person work, or 2 to 4 weeks of residential training depending on severity.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between a reactive dog and an aggressive dog?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reactivity is an over-the-top response to a trigger like another dog or a stranger. Most reactive dogs are fearful or frustrated, not genuinely aggressive. True aggression is rarer and needs careful behavioural assessment.

mainEntity.name: Do you offer residential dog training?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Dogs come from across the UK. The residential programme is the right fit for owners who are time-poor, dogs with severe reactivity, or households where day-to-day management has broken down.

No. We are results-focused. We use whatever ethical, evidence-based method gets the right outcome for the dog in front of us. Same owner, same dog before and after, but a different way of living together.
